items.title: Viral AI fakes flood social media as Iran mourns Khamenei
items.description: Iran drew huge crowds for former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral procession in Tehran, documented by international news agencies. But alongside genuine images pf mourners, AI-generated videos and photos falsely claiming to show the event have spread widely online. Some exaggerate crowd size, while others contain tell-tale AI errors and fabricated landmarks to rack up clicks and engagement.

items.title: Watch: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India | Explained
items.description: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

items.description: Matías Morla tells court Diego Maradona would never have accepted the conditions of the Tigre house where star spent his final days, arguing the home as ill-equipped for a medical emergency. Leer más

items.title: Tanker set ablaze after being struck by projectile in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran mourns Khamenei
items.description: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre said the tanker had been hit near Limah, Oman, in the strait

items.title: Ghana halts visit by South African president amid row over anti-migrant protests
items.description: Many in Ghana feared his presence would lead to mass protests, and officials say it is postponed for now.

items.title: Telstra mobile network crashes nationwide causing transport and payment delays
items.description: Telstra customers, as well as customers of mobile providers that use Telstra's network, have flooded outage detection websites with reports.

items.title: Oral care giant's ads labelled as 'significantly misleading'
items.description: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

items.title: Second Ebola treatment centre opens in North Kivu
items.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) has opened a second Ebola treatment center in North Kivu, one of the three provinces hit by the outbreak, to improve access to specialist care and ease pressure on health facilities.

items.title: NATO summit: Allies in Ankara for talks, defense deals
items.description: The leaders of NATO's 32 member states are holding two days of talks in the Turkish capital. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said the summit aims to ensure the military alliance "continues to deliver." DW has more.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: United States at 250: More prosperous, more diverse, yet more divided
items.description: Demographic change and economic growth have reshaped America over the past 50 years; Immigrants remain the strongest believers in American Dream. Leer más

items.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
items.description: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

items.title: Ex-Syrian intelligence chief found guilty of torture and sexual abuse by Austrian court
items.description: Two former Syrian officials went on trial in Vienna, Austria, accused of crimes against opponents of former Syrian leader Bashar al-Assad

items.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner of sexual assault. And, NATO summit begins
items.description: Graham Platner is facing calls to end his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine after a woman accused him of sexual assault. And, Trump made his dissatisfaction with NATO clear ahead of its summit this week.

items.title: IOC paves way for Russia's return to Olympics
items.description: Russian athletes will be able to take part in qualifying events for the Los Angeles 2028 Olympic Games. It comes two months the IOC had lifted restrictions for Russia's ally Belarus.

items.title: 'We're here to win it again' – Enzo Fernández after Argentina's win over Egypt
items.description: Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni was unable to speak to the media, overcome with emotion and in tears, after thrilling comeback against Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Watch: Moment rare tornado lashes central Chinese cities
items.description: Videos shared with the BBC show debris flying through the air as the storm swept through Ezhou and Huanggang in Hubei province.

items.title: Uruguay veteran Edison Cavani departs Boca Juniors
items.description: Uruguayan striker leaves Boca without having won a title and acknowledges he had "difficult moments" that prevented him from "leaving a mark" on the club. Leer más

items.title: Hamas dissolves its government in Gaza to transfer power to UN-backed committee
items.description: At a news conference on July 6, Ismail al-Thawabta, general director of the Hamas-run Government Media Office, said “only technical and professional staff” would remain in their positions to run the Palestinian enclave’s day-to-day affairs

items.title: France’s Marine Le Pen must wear ankle tag after appeal ruling
items.description: A French appeals court upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing European Parliament funds.

items.title: Trump expected to support potential sale of F-35 jets to Türkiye, sources say
items.description: The news comes just a few weeks after the Trump administration formally notified ‌Congress of its intention to sell jet engines worth more than $700 million for Türkiye’s first indigenous combat jet

items.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
items.description: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

items.title: Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.title: Bandit raid leaves nine farmers dead in northwest Nigeria
items.description: At least nine farmers have been killed and several others abducted in a deadly raid on a farming community in Nigeria's Kaduna State, underscoring the growing insecurity threatening rural livelihoods as armed gangs intensify attacks during the country's planting season.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: North Korea state media says Japan overseas aggression is reality, not hypothetical
items.description: It accused Tokyo of abandoning its long-professed doctrine of exclusively defence-oriented policy and transforming its Military ​into a “thoroughly offensive ‌and aggressive force”

items.title: UK judge dismisses Prince Harry's privacy lawsuit
items.description: The ruling also scuttles breach of privacy suits brought by six others, including Elton John and Elizabeth Hurley.

items.title: No stopping Le Pen? French appeals court clears way for 2027 presidential run
items.description: Did a court decision just alter the outcome a French presidential election that’s less than 300 days in the future? Marine Le Pen’s conviction for funnelling EU parliamentary staff funds to her far-right National Rally party. Watered down by half, her 30-months of ineligibility to run for public office. That makes it now possible for her to launch a fourth bid for the presidency.

items.description: Diego Maradona suffered a sudden change in mood that was downplayed by his psychiatrist during the period of home hospitalisation in which he died, claims nurse in testimony. Leer más

items.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
items.description: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

items.title: Argentina seek to shake off 'Messi-dependence' against Mo Salah's Egypt
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side escaped a major scare against Cape Verde, but their dependence on Lionel Messi remains a growing concern ahead of a last-16 meeting with Mohamed Salah's Egypt. Leer más

items.title: 'It's a very sad day'
items.description: Mexico's World Cup campaign ended with a 3-2 defeat to England in Mexico City.

items.title: Kenyans react to Saba Saba with fear, defiance and calls for justice
items.description: Heavy police deployments and protest bans marked this year's Saba Saba Day in Kenya, but many citizens say the anniversary remains a powerful symbol of the country's unfinished democratic journey. While fear kept crowds small, activists insisted the fight for justice is far from over.

items.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
items.description: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

items.title: Andrés Gil Domínguez: ‘Eliminating the PASO primaries would strip opposition of tool to unify’
items.description: Constitutional lawyer analyses the possible elimination or suspension of the PASO primaries in Argentina, arguing that its possible reform or repeal responds more to political strategy than institutional improvement. Leer más

items.title: UN Secretary-General calls for AI regulation
items.description: The Secretary-General of the United Nations has called for the creation of a global governance system to ensure that artificial intelligence serves humanity, warning against allowing the technology itself to "code our future by feel."

items.title: Argentina pushes US$6 billion in repo maturities past election
items.description: Central Bank extends maturities worth US$6 billion on its repurchase agreements to ease the government debt burden. Leer más

items.title: Almost $100k raised to stop small town slipping into ocean
items.description: Western Australia's opposition says it is outrageous Lancelin residents have been left fundraising to fight erosion slowly consuming the coastal town.

items.title: Experts urge caution as demand grows for AC in heatwave-hit U.K.
items.description: British households opt for AC after second heatwave of 2026 last month

items.title: Tanzania arrests dozens as security tightens ahead of planned protests
items.description: Tanzanian authorities have arrested dozens of people ahead of planned anti-government protests, intensifying a crackdown on dissent as activists demand democratic reforms and the release of jailed opposition leader Tundu Lissu amid heightened security across the country.

items.title: Lionel Messi versus Mohamed Salah in World Cup last-16 showdown
items.description: Argentina survived a huge scare from World Cup first-timers Cape Verde in the last 32. They will hope to resume normal service against Egypt – who have never been beyond the round of 16. Leer más

items.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
items.description: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.
